pysoniq package¶

pysoniq - Lightweight cross-platform audio signal analysis and playback library

pysoniq.adjust_gain_level(audio, gain=None)[source]¶

Adjust audio gain level

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • gain – float, if None uses global gain (1.0 = unity, <1.0 = reduce, >1.0 = boost)

Returns:

audio with gain adjusted (clipped to -1, 1)

pysoniq.amplitude_to_db(S, ref=1.0, amin=1e-10)[source]¶

Convert amplitude spectrogram to dB scale

Parameters:

  • S (np.ndarray) – Amplitude spectrogram

  • ref (float) – Reference amplitude (default: 1.0)

  • amin (float) – Minimum amplitude threshold

Returns:

db – dB spectrogram

Return type:

np.ndarray

pysoniq.compress(audio, threshold_db=-20.0, ratio=4.0, attack_ms=5.0, release_ms=50.0, sr=44100)[source]¶

Simple dynamics compression

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array (mono)

  • threshold_db – float, compression threshold

  • ratio – float, compression ratio (e.g., 4.0 = 4:1)

  • attack_ms – float, attack time in milliseconds

  • release_ms – float, release time in milliseconds

  • sr – int, sample rate

Returns:

compressed audio

pysoniq.db_to_linear(db)[source]¶

Convert dB to linear gain

Parameters:

db – float, decibels

Returns:

linear gain

pysoniq.fft_frequencies(sr=None, n_fft=None, filepath=None)[source]¶

Frequencies corresponding to linear Hz bins.

Parameters:

  • sr (int, optional) – Sample rate in Hz. If None, resolves in order: filepath probe, then module native sr.

  • n_fft (int) – FFT size.

  • filepath (str or Path, optional) – Audio file path. If provided and sr is None, sample rate is extracted.

Returns:

freqs – Frequency array.

Return type:

np.ndarray

Raises:

ValueError – If sr unresolvable (no sr, no filepath, no module native sr), or n_fft is None.

pysoniq.frames_to_time(frames, sr=None, hop_length=None, filepath=None)[source]¶

Convert frame indices to time in seconds.

Parameters:

  • frames (np.ndarray or int) – Frame indices.

  • sr (int, optional) – Sample rate in Hz. If None, filepath must be provided.

  • hop_length (int) – Hop length in samples.

  • filepath (str or Path, optional) – Audio file path. If provided and sr is None, sample rate is extracted.

Returns:

times – Time in seconds.

Return type:

np.ndarray or float

Raises:

ValueError – If both sr and filepath are None, or hop_length is None.

pysoniq.get_gain()[source]¶

Get current global gain (linear)

pysoniq.get_volume_db()[source]¶

Get current global volume in dB

pysoniq.is_looping()[source]¶

Check if looping is enabled

pysoniq.is_paused()[source]¶

Check if paused

pysoniq.limiter(audio, threshold_db=-1.0, ceiling_db=-0.1)[source]¶

Hard limiter to prevent clipping

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • threshold_db – float, where limiting starts

  • ceiling_db – float, absolute maximum level

Returns:

limited audio

pysoniq.linear_to_db(gain)[source]¶

Convert linear gain to dB

Parameters:

gain – float, linear gain

Returns:

dB value

pysoniq.load_signal(filepath)[source]¶
pysoniq.make_audioblobs(source, n_fft=512, metric='srvf', n_classes=None, clustering='kmedoids', min_cluster_size=5, covariance_type='full', m=2.0, gamma=None, feature='raw', n_mfcc=40, aggregation='syllable', random_state=None, return_metadata=False, verbose=False)[source]¶

Build a (X, y) dataset from WAV audio in the shape expected by make_blobs consumers.

Discovers WAV files at source (file or directory), segments each into non-overlapping frames of n_fft samples, applies metric projection, and returns (X, y) where X is (n_samples, n_fft) and y is integer class labels. Class labels are derived from the directory tree under source. Optionally subsample to n_classes if fewer classes are needed.

Parameters:

  • source (path to a single WAV file or a directory of WAV files.)

  • n_fft (frame length in samples. SR is read natively from each file.)

  • metric (one of 'euclidean', 'cosine', 'wasserstein', 'srvf'.)

  • n_classes (if set, subsample to this many classes randomly.)

  • random_state (seed or RandomState for reproducibility.)

  • verbose (enable verbose debug logging.)

  • clustering (str)

  • min_cluster_size (int)

  • covariance_type (str)

  • m (float)

  • gamma (float | None)

  • feature (str)

  • n_mfcc (int)

  • aggregation (str | None)

  • return_metadata (bool)

Returns:

  • X (np.ndarray, shape (n_samples, n_fft))

  • y (np.ndarray, shape (n_samples,), integer class labels)

Return type:

tuple[ndarray, ndarray] | tuple[ndarray, ndarray, dict]

pysoniq.mel_filterbank(sr, n_fft, n_mels=128, fmin=0.0, fmax=None)[source]¶

Construct a triangular mel filterbank matrix.

Returns a matrix of shape (n_mels, n_fft // 2 + 1) mapping linear FFT bins to mel-spaced filter outputs. Reference: O’Shaughnessy (1987). Speech Communication. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1016/0167-6393(87)90050-3

Parameters:

  • sr (int)

  • n_fft (int)

  • n_mels (int)

  • fmin (float)

  • fmax (float)

Return type:

ndarray

pysoniq.mfcc(y, sr, n_mfcc=40, n_mels=128, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, fmin=0.0, fmax=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Compute Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) from a waveform.

Pipeline: STFT -> power spectrogram -> mel filterbank -> log -> DCT -> n_mfcc coefficients. Returns array of shape (n_mfcc, n_frames).

Parameters:

  • y (ndarray)

  • sr (int)

  • n_mfcc (int)

  • n_mels (int)

  • n_fft (int)

  • hop_length (int)

  • fmin (float)

  • fmax (float)

  • window (str)

Return type:

ndarray

pysoniq.normalize(audio, target_db=-3.0)[source]¶

Normalize audio to target dB level

Parameters:

  • audio – numpy array

  • target_db – float, target peak level in dB (e.g., -3.0)

Returns:

normalized audio

pysoniq.pause()[source]¶

Pause current playback

pysoniq.play(audio_data, samplerate=None, blocking=False)[source]¶

Play audio from file or array

Parameters:

  • audio_data – str/Path (audio file) or numpy array (audio audio_data)

  • samplerate – int, required if audio_data is array

  • blocking – bool, if True wait for playback to finish

pysoniq.resume()[source]¶

Resume playback from pause point

pysoniq.set_gain(gain)[source]¶

Set global gain (linear)

Parameters:

gain – float, 0.0 to 2.0+ (1.0 = unity)

pysoniq.set_loop(enabled)[source]¶

Enable or disable looping

pysoniq.set_volume_db(db)[source]¶

Set global volume in dB

Parameters:

db – float, typically -60 to +6 dB (0 dB = unity)

pysoniq.stft(y, n_fft=1024, hop_length=None, window='hann')[source]¶

Complex-valued short-time Fourier transform; Zxx is a complex-valued matrix that retains magnitude and phase information

Parameters:

  • y (np.ndarray) – Audio time series

  • n_fft (int) – FFT window size

  • hop_length (int or None) – Number of samples between frames

  • window (str) – Window type (‘hann’, ‘hamming’, ‘blackman’)

Returns:

stft_matrix – STFT matrix

Return type:

np.ndarray (complex)

pysoniq.stop()[source]¶

Stop audio playback (preserves loop state)
